---
title: "About Me"
---

I'm a 1st-class Geography BSc graduate from the University of Manchester with a specialism in GIS and spatial analysis. My dissertation — *Please Marten the Gap* — developed a Python-based spatial prioritisation framework for red squirrel conservation, combining species interaction modelling with multi-criteria decision analysis to guide reserve selection under pine marten recolonisation scenarios in Cumbria.

I'm looking for opportunities in GIS-informed consultation or technical roles. My interests span planning, landscape ecology and biodiversity net gain (BNG), and sustainable development. Opportunities aligned with these goals are always welcome.

---

## Skills

- **Geospatial programming:** Python (GeoPandas, Shapely, Rasterio, PyProj, scikit-image), spatial indexing (R-trees), raster and vector workflows
- **GIS platforms:** ArcGIS Pro, QGIS
- **Analysis:** Multi-criteria decision analysis, landscape ecology metrics, algorithm design, data visualisation (Matplotlib)
- **Other:** Git, Quarto, reproducible research workflows

---

## Contact

You can find all my code on [GitHub](https://github.com/MaxLarner/gis-portfolio-max-larner), connect with me on [LinkedIn](https://linkedin.com/in/max-larner-b53610208), or reach me by email at max.larner123@gmail.com.
